data.title: Genius Group increases Bitcoin treasury to 172 Bitcoin
data.description:
• Genius Group Limited (NYSE:GNS) announced an increase in its Bitcoin purchases for its Bitcoin Treasury by an additional $1.8M. 
  • The company now holds 172 Bitcoin, acquired for a total of $15.8M, at an average net price of $92,006 per Bitcoin.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Kulicke and Soffa Industries completes $800M share repurchase program
data.description:
• Kulicke and Soffa Industries (NASDAQ:KLIC) completed its $800M share repurchase program, and has expanded its shareholder return activities with a new $300M stock buyback program.
  • KLIC shares were trading -1.80% Monday pre-market at $47.55.
  • The semiconductor and electronics assembly solutions company repurchased ~22.7M shares at about $35.25 per share.
  • Additionally, the company has again expanded its shareholder return activities with a new $300M stock buyback program.
  • Kulicke and Soffa may repurchase shares of common stock through open market and privately negotiated transactions at prices deemed appropriate by the management.
  • The company said it has deployed $900M to repurchase a total of 30.9M shares at an average price of ~$29.12 per share since the announcement of its initial repurchase program on Aug. 27, 2014.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Psychemedics announces 1-for-5,000 reverse and forward stock split
data.description:
• Psychemedics Corporation (NASDAQ:PMD) approved a 1-for-5,000 reverse stock split of its common stock followed immediately by a 5,000-for-1 forward stock split of its common stock to be effective at 5:01 p.m. and 5:02 p.m.,ET, respectively, on Tuesday, December 3, 2024.
  • Beginning with the opening of trading on Wednesday, December 4, 2024, the company’s common stock will continue to trade on the Nasdaq Capital Market on a post-Forward Stock Split basis under the existing symbol “PMD”.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Gelteq’s largest U.S. customer increases initial order by 50%
data.description:
• Gelteq Limited (NASDAQ:GELS) said on Monday that its largest U.S. customer, Healthy Extracts (OTCQB:HYEX), has increased its initial purchase order of Gelteq collagen and Mynus sugar blocker gels by 50%, citing high demand and popularity of the products.
  • Healthy Extracts’ platform acquires, develops, patents, markets, and distributes plant-based nutraceuticals that target select high-growth categories within the nutraceuticals market.
  • Production of Healthy Extracts’ order is underway, with shipment expected in January 2025.
  • Gelteq Limited (GELS) shares were up more than 10% in pre-market trading on Monday.

data.title: CEMATRIX announces Randy Boomhour as president and CEO
data.description:
• CEMATRIX Corporation (OTCQB:CTXXF) announced that Randy Boomhour has been named president and chief executive officer effective November 29, 2024.
  • Boomhour joined CEMATRIX in 2020 as finance chief and was promoted to chief operating officer in 2024.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Weekly economic calendar: Focus on Jobs Report, JOLTS, ISM Service Index
data.description:
Monday - December 2

9:45 AM PMI Manufacturing Final

The Manufacturing Purchasing Managers' Index provides survey results from manufacturing firms throughout the country. Based on a flash PMI of 48.8, the economist expects the manufacturing sector to rise in November, up from 48.5 in October.

10:00 AM ISM Manufacturing Index

The Institute of Supply Management Manufacturing Purchasing Managers Index Report on Business is based on data compiled from monthly replies to questions asked of purchasing and supply executives in over 400 industrial companies across the United States. The November ISM Manufacturing PMI is expected to be 47.6, 1.1 points higher than the prior month, although the read still remains in contraction territory.

10:00 AM Construction Spending

The Construction Spending index measures the change in the total amount spent on construction (both public and private). Forecasters see a marginal rise of 0.2% increase on the month.

3:15 PM Fed's Waller: Economic Outlook

Federal Reserve Board Governor Christopher Waller to speak on the economic outlook before the American Institute for Economic Research Monetary Conference,"Building a Better Fed Framework"

4:30 PM Fed's Williams Speech

New York Federal Reserve Bank President John Williams to participate in a conversation organized by the Queens Chamber of Commerce.

Tuesday - December 3

Auto Sales

10:00 AM Job Openings and Labor Turnover Survey

A survey done by the US Bureau of Labor Statistics collects data from employers about their businesses' employment, job openings, recruitment, hires and separations. October job openings are expected to be 7.49 million from 7.443 million in September.

11:00 AM Treasury Buyback Announcement (Preliminary)

The Treasury conducts buybacks to improve liquidity for certain maturities, limit variations in auction sizes, and reduce maturity peaks in outstanding debt.

12:35 PM Fed’s Kugler: "Labor Market and Monetary Policy"

Federal Reserve Board Governor Adriana Kugler to speak on "Labor Market and Monetary Policy" before the Detroit Economic Club.

3:45 PM Fed’s Goolsbee: "The Changing Landscape for Agricultural Inputs"

Chicago Federal Reserve Bank President Austan Goolsbee to give closing remarks before hybrid Midwest Agriculture Conference, "The Changing Landscape for Agricultural Inputs"

Wednesday - December 4

07:00 AM MBA Mortgage Applications

The data point measures the change in the number of new applications for mortgages backed by the MBA during the reported week. Mortgage applications rose for the third week, ending 6.3% higher for the week ending November 27 after the benchmark 30-year fixed mortgage rate fell to 6.86%.

08:15 AM ADP Jobs Report

The ADP National Employment Report is a measure of the monthly change in non-farm, private employment, based on the payroll data of approximately 400,000 U.S. business clients. Private businesses in the US are expected to add 165K in November after adding 233K workers to their payrolls in October 2024.

8:45 AM Fed’s Musalem Speech

St. Louis Federal Reserve Bank President Alberto Musalem to speak before the Bloomberg/Global Interdependence Center College of Central Bankers Symposium.

9:45 PMI Composite Final

In the United States, Markit Composite PMI Output Index tracks business trends across both manufacturing and service sectors. The index is based on data collected from a representative panel of over 1,000 companies and follows variables such as sales, new orders, employment, inventories and prices. The S&P Global Flash US Composite PMI rose to 55.3 in November 2024 from 54.1 in October.

10:00 Factory Orders

Factory Orders measures the change in the total value of new purchase orders placed with manufacturers. October new orders are expected to rise 0.4% after a drop of 0.5% in September.

10:00 ISM Service Index

The ISM Report On Business continues to be consistent and accurate in indicating the direction of the overall economy, in addition to the manufacturing and services sectors. The analyst expects ISM Services PMI to come in at 55.5 in November, down from 56.0 in October reading.

10:30 AM EIA Petroleum Inventories

The Energy Information Administration weekly data reports inventory levels of US crude oil, gasoline and distillates stocks. The figure shows how much oil and products are available in storage. U.S. commercial crude oil inventories decreased by 1.84 million barrels in the last week.

11:00 AM Treasury Buyback Announcement

Buybacks are a cash management tool to give the government flexibility to manage the public debt.

11:00 AM Treasury Buyback Announcement (Preliminary)

The Treasury conducts buybacks to improve liquidity for certain maturities, limit variations in auction sizes, and reduce maturity peaks in outstanding debt.

1:45 PM Jerome Powell Speech

Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell to participate in moderated discussion before the New York Times DealBook Summit.

2:00 PM Fed's Beige Book

The Federal Reserve's Beige Book is a report on current economic conditions in each of the 12 Federal districts in the U.S. It gives a picture of economic trends and challenges in the U.S. It is released 8 times a year, 2 weeks before each Federal Open Market Committee meeting. The report is used by the FOMC in their decision on short-term interest rates.

2:00 PM Treasury Buyback Results

Institutions that are approved by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York (FRBNY) to conduct open market transactions may participate in a buyback. FRBNY accepts offers with the best value relative to prevailing market prices. Shortly after the operation, Treasury publishes summary results.

Thursday - December 5

7:30 Challenger jobs-Cut Report

This monthly report counts and categorizes announcements of corporate layoffs based on mass layoff data from state departments of labor 49K job cuts are expected in November after US employers announced 55,597 job cuts in October 2024.

8:30 Initial Jobless Claims

Initial Jobless Claims measures the number of individuals who filed for unemployment insurance for the first time during the past week. The jobless claims is expected to edge higher to 215K after holding steady at 213K.

8:30 International Trade in Goods and Services

Trade in goods and services between U.S. residents and residents of other countries each month. U.S. sales are exports and U.S. purchases are imports. The difference between the exports and imports is the trade balance. The trade balance deficit is expected to narrow to $75.4B in October after widening to $84.40B in September.

10:30 AM EIA Natural Gas Report

The Energy Information Administration Natural Gas Storage report measures the change in the number of cubic feet of natural gas held in underground storage over the prior week. US utilities withdraw 2 billion cubic feet of gas into storage during the week that ended November 27, 2024.

11:00 AM Treasury Buyback Announcement

Buybacks are a cash management tool to give the government flexibility to manage the public debt.

11:30 AM Fed’s Barkin Speech

Richmond Federal Reserve Bank President Thomas Barkin to speak before in-person Charlotte Regional Business Alliance 2024 Economic Outlook event.

2:00 PM Treasury Buyback Results

Institutions that are approved by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York (FRBNY) to conduct open market transactions may participate in a buyback. FRBNY accepts offers with the best value relative to prevailing market prices. Shortly after the operation, Treasury publishes summary results.

4:30 PM Fed Balance Sheet

The Fed balance sheet is a weekly report listing the assets and liabilities of the Federal Reserve System.

Friday - December 6

8:30 Non-farm payrolls

Nonfarm payrolls track the number of part-time and full-time employees in both business and government. Economists expect 200K job additions for the month and for the unemployment rate to hold steady at 4.2%.

9:15 AM Fed’s Bowman Speech

Federal Reserve Board Governor Michelle Bowman to participate virtually in discussion before the Missouri Bankers Association Executive Management Conference.

10:00 AM Consumer Sentiment

The University of Michigan Consumer Sentiment Index is composed of survey results comparing the relative level of both current and future economic conditions. There are two versions of this data released two weeks apart, preliminary and revised. The preliminary data tends to have a greater impact as it is more timely. The reading is compiled from a survey of around 500 consumers. The December preliminary print is expected at 73.0.

10:30 AM Fed’s Goolsbee Speech

Chicago Federal Reserve Bank President Austan Goolsbee to participate in fireside chat before hybrid 38th Annual Economic Outlook Symposium hosted by the Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago.

11:00 AM Treasury Buyback Announcement (Preliminary)

The Treasury conducts buybacks to improve liquidity for certain maturities, limit variations in auction sizes, and reduce maturity peaks in outstanding debt.

12:00 PM Fed’s Hammack: Economic Outlook

Cleveland Federal Reserve Bank President Beth Hammack to speak on the economic outlook before the City Club of Cleveland Friday Forum

1:00 PM Baker Hughes Rig Count

The Baker Hughes North American rig count tracks weekly changes in the number of active operating oil & gas rigs. The active rig count acts as a leading indicator of demand for oil products. Crude Oil Rigs in the United States decreased to 477 on November 27 from 479 in the previous week.

1:00 PM Fed’s Daly: "Emerging Technology and the Economy"

San Francisco Federal Reserve Bank President Mary Daly to participate in "Emerging Technology and the Economy" conversation hosted by the Hoover Institution.

3:00 PM US Consumer Credit

Consumer Credit measures the change in the total value of outstanding consumer credit in the United States.

data.title: Castor Maritime sells M/V Ariana A for $16.5M
data.description:
• Castor Maritime (NASDAQ:CTRM) said through a separate unit, it entered into a sales agreement for the M/V Ariana A for $16.5 million.
  • The vessel is expected to be delivered during the first quarter of 2025.
  • The firm expects to report a net sale of $3.3M in the first quarter of 2025 from the sale.

data.title: Vision Marine Technologies surges on production partnership with Massimo Marine
data.description:
• Vision Marine Technologies (NASDAQ:VMAR) entered into a production partnership with Massimo Group's (NASDAQ:MAMO) marine division, Massimo Marine
  • The collaboration is to produce a fully integrated 30-foot electric pontoon platform designed for commercial and recreational markets.
  • Massimo Marine will supply pontoon boats to be equipped with Vision Marine's E-Motion 180E Powertrain System.
  • VMAR shares were up over 12% in premarket trading on Monday.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Medicus Pharma collaborates to expand mid-stage cancer treatment study in Asia-Pacific region
data.description:
• Medicus Pharma (NASDAQ:MDCX) announced an agreement with Swanielle to explore expansion of Phase 2 clinical study for treatment of basal cell carcinoma in the Asia-pacific region.
  • Under the agreement, Swanielle, a healthcare consulting firm, will help identify a pharmaceutical/biotech company or companies in Southeast Asia to partner with Medicus to expand the mid-stage study in the Asia Pacific region.
  • The company also said that the study is currently underway in nine clinical sites in the United States and has already randomized more than 25% of the 60 patients expected to be enrolled in the study.
  • Medicus is planning to initiate an interim data analysis in Q1 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Nu Holdings cut to Sell at Citi as growth trajectory challenged
data.description:
Citi downgraded Nu Holdings (NYSE:NU), which operates NuBank in Brazil and Mexico, to Sell from Neutral as it faces a tougher road for growth after rapid expansion.

Nu Holdings (NYSE:NU) stock dipped 2.2% in Monday premarket trading.

Q3 2024 saw a combination of lower average revenue per active customer Q/Q, higher cost of funding, and Q/Q decline in net interest income, mainly in credit cards, analyst Gustavo Schroden wrote in a note to clients.

In addition, risk-adjusted net interest margin fell 90 basis points Q/Q. "As a result, its pretax earnings remain virtually stable Q/Q after nine consecutive quarters of expansion," Schroden said.

He expects it will take longer than expected for alternative revenue sources, such as payroll loans in Brazil, Mexico, and Colombia, to offset the slowdown in credit cards and personal loans in Brazil.

Meanwhile, Citi reiterates its Buy rating for Inter & Co. (INTR), noting that the company is still in the early stage of growth in unsecured loans and its diversification efforts could support even faster growth in NII/NIM.

Schroden's Sell rating is even more bearish than the SA Quant rating of Hold and clashes with the average SA Analyst and average Wall Street ratings, both at Buy.
